title: Jekyll, Chirpy and the Github Pages
tags: ruby, bundle, blog, blogging, host, hosting, jekyll, chirpy, theme, jekyll-theme, github, github-pages
snippet: Github Pages lets you host Jekyll sites and Chirpy makes it even easier.Photo by Florian Bernhardt on UnsplashPrerequisitesMake sure to have Ruby installed.On Mac:moncefbelyamani’s tutorial.On Windowshttps://rubyinstaller.org/Steps Create a new repository from the Chirpy Starter and name it &lt;GH_USERNAME&gt;.github.io if you want to host with Github Pages at https://&lt;GH_USERNAME&gt;.github.io. If you want to host at https://&lt;GH_USERNAME&gt;.github.io/&lt;REPO_NAME&gt;, then just name the repo whatever you want. Cloned the repo locally. Installed dependencies with command bundle from both windows and mac. Also ran: bundle lock --add-platform x86_64-linux In _config.yml, updated baseurl to &lt;REPO_NAME&gt; updated url to https://&lt;GH_USERNAME&gt;.github.io Then pushed to main branch. That triggered the /.github/workflows/pages-deploy.yml and the branch gh-pages on github was automatically created and site build was also successful. Then went to repository on GitHub &gt; Settings &gt; Pages and as Source, chose gh-pages as the branch and /(root) as the root folder. Shortly afterwards, the site became accessible.
